Find out what SHOULD be under your Chris.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Best Christmas Hiking Gifts & Epic Adventures Wrap-Up
Find out what SHOULD be under your Chris.spotify_description:
Join Blair Woodcock, along with Townsville Bushwalking Club experts Cherry Judge and Luen Warneke, for our ultimate end-of-year holiday special! With Christmas just around the corner, the team is here to save your festive shopping with a comprehensive gift guide for the outdoor enthusiast in your life. Whether you are shopping for a seasoned hiker or dropping hints for your own stocking, we have you covered with the best gear recommendations to hit the trails in North Queensland and beyond.


In this episode, we dive into the top items every hiker wants, from foolproof dry bags and high-quality headlamps to lightweight titanium thermoses and hammock tents. Luen shares a clever fuel transfer gadget, while Cherry hints at wanting a solar lantern for car camping. We also discuss practical lifesavers like snake bandages, water sterilization tabs, and power banks. Plus, if you are looking to splurge, find out why GPS watches and camping permits are making the list this year!


Finally, we wrap up the year by reflecting on our favorite bushwalking adventures of 2022, including the Atherton waterfall circuit and packrafting down Stony Creek. We also look ahead to epic 2023 plans, from Tasmania's Overland Track to Blair's ambitious 500-kilometer solo hike in the USA. Grab your favorite hiking socks, settle in, and get ready for a massive year of adventure. For more info, check out the blog at [https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/christmas-gift/](https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/christmas-gift/) and have a safe and happy holiday!


Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/christmas-gift/

Take a Hike - A family friendly hike and.episode_file: Take a Hike - A family friendly hike and what to do if you have to do a number 2.mp3
Take a Hike - A family friendly hike and.youtube_title: Townsville's Hidden Waterfall Hike & How to Poo in the Bush! | Big Crystal Creek Guide

Take a Hike - A family friendly hike and.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Big Crystal Creek Hike & Trail Toilet Etiquette
Take a Hike - A family friendly hike and.spotify_description:
Join Blair Woodcock, Cherry Judge, and Luen Warneke for another episode of Take A Hike, your guide to the best bushwalking trails around Townsville. This week, we are taking a break from the massive ascents to bring you a relaxing, family-friendly half-day adventure at Big Crystal Creek. Starting from the Mango Tree Car Park in Paluma, Luen guides us through a scenic, low-elevation route featuring secret waterfalls, boulder hopping, and plenty of swimming spots.


But before we dive into the cool waters of Cloudy and Ethel Creek, we have to talk about a taboo but entirely necessary topic: doing a number two in the bush. The crew shares their favorite scenic toilet locations from Tasmania to the Great Ocean Walk. More importantly, we cover essential wilderness toilet etiquette. Learn why you need to remember the numbers '200' and '15', the great toilet paper debate, and why portable bidets might just be the future of ultralight hiking.


Whether you are planning a relaxed weekend hike at Big Crystal Creek or preparing for a multi-day remote trek, this episode is packed with laughs, tips, and trail inspiration. Grab your map, your swimmers, and your travel trowel, and let's hit the trails! Plus, hear what the Townsville Bushwalking Club has planned next for Killymoon Creek and Mount Flagstone.


Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/big-crystal-creek-family-hike-toilet-etiquette/

Take a Hike - Are Pack Liners needed_ an.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Pack Liners & Mitch Nissan's Godzone Hiking Adventure
Take a Hike - Are Pack Liners needed_ an.spotify_description:
Welcome back to Take a Hike, your guide to the best bushwalking and hiking trails around Townsville and North Queensland! This week, Blair Woodcock, Cherry Judge, and Luen Warneke dive into the great gear debate: do you really need a pack liner for your backpack, or are individual dry bags the way to go? The team shares their personal waterproofing strategies for keeping gear dry while hitting the creeks and trails.

Then, we are joined by avid adventure racer Mitch Nissan, who recently moved to Townsville. Mitch shares his incredible journey tackling one of the world's toughest expedition races, Godzone, in New Zealand's rugged Fiordland. Discover what it takes to survive 12,000 meters of elevation and over 500 kilometers of trekking, pack rafting, and mountain biking on just a few hours of broken sleep. Mitch recounts his team's massive push to overcome a 4-hour navigation error in the dead of night and why their North Queensland paddling background gave them a competitive edge.

Tune in for an inspiring chat about extreme endurance, teamwork, and navigating the wild without a GPS. Plus, hear a sneak peek of next week's episode, where Mitch will break down his favorite local trails and his very own Grin and Bear Race at Lake Tinaroo. Grab your gear and let's take a hike!

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/pack-liners-and-mitch-nissan-godzone/

Take a Hike - Are there any cassowaries.spotify_description:
Welcome back to "Take A Hike"! This week, Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge dive into a local mystery: are there actually cassowaries in Paluma? The crew discusses recent sightings, how to identify their unique droppings on the trail, and how you can contribute to the Cassowary Citizen Science Project supported by the Townsville Bushwalking Club.


Next, we hit the trail to Godwin's Peak. Known as one of the best summits in the Paluma Range National Park, this challenging track offers breathtaking 360-degree views of the coastline. We break down the best ways to tackle the hike, from a leisurely walk to Twin Falls and Pick Falls, to the steep eight-hour trek to the grassy summit. We also cover access tips near Toomulla, how to avoid missing the summit turn-off, and how to link your hike with Mount Halifax for the ultimate North Queensland adventure. Grab your map and GPS, and let's get out there!


Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/cassowaries-paluma-godwins-peak/

Take a Hike - Best Waterfalls and Couple.episode_file: Take a Hike - Best Waterfalls and Couples Adventures.mp3
Take a Hike - Best Waterfalls and Couple.youtube_title: Hiking Townsville: Best Waterfalls & Wild Couple Adventures (Leeches & Sharks!)

Take a Hike - Best Waterfalls and Couple.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Best Waterfalls & Couples Hiking Adventures
Take a Hike - Best Waterfalls and Couple.spotify_description:
Welcome back to Take A Hike! This week, Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge dive into the age-old debate: what actually makes the perfect waterfall? Whether it's the journey, the drop, or the hidden caves, the Townsville bushwalking crew shares their top picks, including a stunning hidden gem in the Russell River area and some international favorites.

We are also thrilled to welcome a very special guest to the studio... Professor Jody Rummer, marine biologist and Luen's partner in adventure! Jody shares how she transitioned from sports physiology to studying sharks, and gives us an inside look at what it's like tackling extreme couples adventures. From stripping off hundreds of leeches on a razorback ridge to romantic (and terrifying) first dates canyoning down Jourama Falls and night climbing Castle Hill, Jody spills the truth on surviving Luen's grandiose hiking plans.

Plus, we chat about the beautiful contrasting landscapes of the Townsville region, from the lush rainforests to the rugged terrain of Bowling Green Bay National Park. Whether you are into drift diving with sharks, trail running on Magnetic Island, or just looking for your next weekend bushwalking trip, this episode is packed with wild stories and outdoor inspiration.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/best-waterfalls-couples-adventures/

Take a Hike - Discover some secret falls.spotify_title: Bushwalking Townsville: Discover Secret Falls & Hiking Wooroonooran
Take a Hike - Discover some secret falls.spotify_description:
Looking for your next long weekend adventure away from the crowds? In this episode of "Take A Hike", Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge uncover some hidden gems for your next bushwalking trip. We venture north of Townsville to the lush, untouched rainforests of Wooroonooran National Park, exploring secret spots you won't find on standard trail maps.

Join the Townsville Bushwalking Club as they share the inside scoop on reaching Cowley Falls and Friendship Falls. Learn how to navigate the old, overgrown tracks, climb steep ridge lines, and even peer out from a secret cave behind an 80-meter waterfall. Plus, the crew shares a crucial warning about unexpected local wildlife in the South Johnston River - yes, we are talking about estuarine crocodiles in the Misty Mountains!

If you are setting up at the Henrietta Camping Area, you will also get the details on another unknown spot, Tipun Falls, before we wrap up with a quick chat about weekend hiking plans at Cape Cleveland. Grab your gear, stay croc-wise, and discover the best off-grid trails North Queensland has to offer!

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/discover-secret-falls-wooroonooran/

Take a Hike - Discover some small advent.spotify_description:
Looking to escape the Townsville heat and humidity this long weekend? Join Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club as they share the perfect one-day hiking adventures just an hour away in beautiful Paluma. Whether you left your accommodation booking too late or just want to burn off those Easter chocolate eggs, these easy day trips are the answer to your prayers.

In this episode of Take A Hike, the crew explores an array of beginner-friendly and introductory off-track walks. Cherry highlights the family-friendly Ethel Creek Falls and shares her tips for a refreshing, creek-wading adventure through Big and Little Birthday Creek. Blair talks up the stunning valley views from Witts Lookout, while Luen reveals the secret to finding Mossy Falls, a photographer's dream just off the dam road.

Grab your map, pack your insect repellent, and maybe leave the fancy hiking shoes at home if you plan on getting your feet wet! Tune in to hear all the trail details, plus find out where the team is heading for their own outdoor getaways. Happy hiking!

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/paluma-small-adventures/

Take a Hike - Expore from the Paluma Dam.spotify_description:
Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club experts Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge for another adventure in North Queensland! With the humidity dropping, it's the perfect time to hit the trails. In this episode, the team debates the wild world of ultralight hiking versus traditional backpacking. From debating standard base weights to wild minimalist setups, Luen and Cherry share their tips for balancing trail comfort with camp comfort without breaking your back.

We also map out an epic local hiking route: the journey from Paluma Dam down to Jourama Falls. Discover what to expect along this scenic track, including historical tin mining artifacts, the beautiful Station Creek, and a must-do side trip to Black Hole Falls. Whether you are tackling it as a gruelling day trip or a relaxing multi-day bushwalking experience, this trail offers some of the best landscapes the region has to offer.

Plus, get all the details on the Townsville Bushwalking Club's upcoming guided trek in July with local legend Wilfred Karnoll. The crew also shares info about their recent community stall at the local 4WD expo. Pack your bags, weigh your gear, and get ready to take a hike! Find more details at townsvillebushwalkingclub.com.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/paluma-dam-jourama-falls-pack-weight/

Take a Hike - GeoTagging Debate and Porc.spotify_description:
Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club regulars Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they navigate the complexities of sharing our favorite trails online. In this episode, we dive into the heated debate surrounding geotagging in the age of social media. Is it responsible to tag remote locations, or does it invite unintended environmental damage and safety risks? Our experts weigh the benefits of raising awareness against the realities of overcrowding, litter, and the need for trail preservation.

After tackling the debate, we shift our focus to the stunning landscapes of Porcupine Gorge. Perfect for an upcoming long weekend, this spectacular gorge offers a rugged and rewarding experience for those ready to explore. From navigating water levels and remote bushwalking to essential tips on safety and logistics, we share everything you need to know about planning your next adventure in this North Queensland gem. Whether you are an experienced hiker or looking for your next scenic trip, this episode is packed with insights to help you hike smarter and safer.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/geotagging-debate-porcupine-gorge/

Take a Hike - Hear About Some Long Hikes.spotify_description:
Planning your next Townsville bushwalking adventure? Join host Blair Woodcock along with guests Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they dive into the perfect escapes for the upcoming long weekend. This episode highlights the stunning Wallaman Falls in Girringun National Park, exploring everything from the spectacular scenery and camping opportunities to the lesser-known walking tracks and hidden swimming holes in the area. Whether you are looking for a simple day trip or an extended multi-day challenge along the Great Walks, our experts share their top tips for navigating this wild and beautiful landscape.

Beyond the trails, the team discusses the vital importance of Leave No Trace principles. They break down why building rock cairns can be harmful to fragile ecosystems and discuss the ethics of keeping our natural spaces wild. Tune in to pick up some expert advice on how to explore responsibly, leave only footprints behind, and make the most of your time in North Queensland's great outdoors.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/long-weekend-hikes-townsville-wallaman/

Take a Hike - Here are a few local hikes.spotify_description:
School holidays are here, and it is the perfect time to get the family outdoors! In this episode of Take a Hike, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Townsville Bushwalking Club regulars Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to discuss the best local trails to keep kids entertained. Whether you are looking for a quick, scenic stroll to a lookout or a full-day adventure, we cover a range of options in the Paluma Range and surrounding regions that are sure to tire the little ones out while creating lasting memories in nature.

From the refreshing swimming holes at Birthday Creek Falls and Cloudy Creek to the stunning views at Witt's Lookout, our guests share their expert tips for family-friendly hiking. We also dive into some of the more challenging routes, such as the trek to Paluma Ridge Diorite Falls and the historic DCK Shelter. We wrap up with advice on where to find reliable maps and essential planning tips to ensure your bushwalking adventure is safe, fun, and filled with great discoveries.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/local-hikes-to-entertain-kids-paluma/

With public holiday season approaching, it is time to start planning your escape! Whether you are looking for a relaxing getaway or an off-road adventure, the Easter long weekend is the perfect excuse to get out and explore North Queensland. In this episode, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Cherry Judge and Luen Warneke from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to discuss their top camping recommendations, covering everything from easily accessible car-camping sites to more adventurous, remote locations that require a bit of extra planning.

From the tranquil waters of Paluma Dam and the family-friendly Broadwater to the rugged, high-clearance trails of Blencoe and the coastal beauty of Salmon Creek, our panel breaks down the best spots for your upcoming holiday. They share insider tips on where to go, what to pack, and how to make the most of your trip while navigating the busy holiday crowds. Whether you are hitting the road or heading out on the water, tune in for expert advice to ensure your Easter break is one to remember.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/camping-ideas-easter-long-weekend/

Take a Hike - Hiking Hygiene & More with.spotify_description:
Welcome back to "Take A Hike", your guide to the best bushwalking and hiking trails around Townsville and North Queensland! Join host Blair Woodcock, along with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club, as we dive into a topic every outdoor enthusiast needs to know: trail hygiene. From the Leave No Trace principles of washing dishes in streams to the great debate on wet wipes and toilet paper alternatives (warm rocks, anyone?), Luen shares his top tips for staying clean without harming the environment.

In the second half of our chat with extreme adventure racer and paddler Mitch Nissan, we explore his favourite local spots like Clement State Forest and recount some truly wild off-track bushwalking stories. Hear how Luen and Mitch first crossed paths near the summit of Mount Pieter Botte, and cringe at the tale of tackling hundreds of leeches on a hike over Mount Bartle Frere.

We also look ahead to some exciting local events on the North Queensland calendar. Get the scoop on the Paluma Push mountain bike race, Mitch's upcoming 250km multi-sport water race in Sweden, and how you can register for the Grin and Bear It tournament at Lake Tinaroo. Whether you love to paddle, run, trek, or swim, there is an adventure waiting for you! Head to townsvillebushwalkingclub.com to start your next journey.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/hiking-hygiene-mitch-nissan/

Take a Hike - Magnetic Island.mp3.spotify_title: Hiking Magnetic Island: From Coastal Trails to Hidden Summits
Take a Hike - Magnetic Island.mp3.spotify_description:
Ready to explore the hidden gems of North Queensland's Magnetic Island? In this episode of Take a Hike, your host Blair Woodcock sits down with Townsville Bushwalking Club experts Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to uncover the best trails on 'Maggie'. Whether you are looking for an easy, sunset-friendly stroll or a challenging off-track scramble to a hidden summit, this island has it all. We break down must-do walks including the scenic Hawkings Point, the ridge trail at Nellie Bay, and the historic path leading to the forts.

Beyond the popular spots, we dive into the island's fascinating history, from World War II searchlight towers to the newly restored paths leading down to pristine bays. Cherry and Luen share their top tips for navigating the terrain safely, including advice on how to tackle the more adventurous, unmarked routes for those with solid navigation skills. If you are planning your next weekend adventure in the Townsville region, this guide to Magnetic Island's diverse hiking network is the perfect place to start.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/magnetic-island-hiking-guide/

North Queensland's wet season has arrived, turning our local creeks into vibrant, rushing waterways. In this episode, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to discuss how to safely embrace these changes. From navigating challenging off-track adventures like Saddle Creek to understanding the thrill and risks of paddling local rivers, we explore how to turn the wet weather into an unforgettable outdoor experience.

Beyond the adventure, we dive into essential safety and gear advice. Whether you are planning a creek-based trek or a casual swim, our experts break down the best ways to waterproof your gear, discuss the importance of proper navigation, and offer tips on avoiding flash floods. Tune in to hear how you can join the Townsville Bushwalking Club on their upcoming trips and gain the experience needed to explore the best of our region's hidden cascades and swimming holes safely.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/townsville-wet-season-hikes-waterproofing-gear/

Take a Hike - Map Vs GPS & Take a hike t.spotify_description:
Ever found yourself staring at a screen, hoping the blue dot on your phone doesn't steer you wrong? In this episode of Take a Hike, Blair Woodcock joins Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to debate the age-old dilemma of modern technology versus traditional navigation. We break down the pros and cons of using GPS apps like AllTrails or Gaia versus trusting your skills with a map and compass. Whether you are navigating dense rainforest canopy or open terrain, we discuss why having both skills in your kit is crucial for getting out of trouble when the battery dies.

After our deep dive into navigation, we take a trip to the rugged landscapes near the Ross River Dam. We share a recent Townsville Bushwalking Club adventure to the elusive Mount Flagstone, a stunning peak often spotted on the drive to Charters Towers but rarely explored due to its private property access. From the challenges of off-trail walking to discovering historical trig points and the unique flora of the Mingela State Forest, this episode is packed with inspiration for your next North Queensland bushwalking adventure.

Take a Hike - Talking F U Wait & Special.spotify_description:
Ever felt the sting of the 'F.U. Wait' on a hike? Join host Blair Woodcock and the Townsville Bushwalking Club crew, Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge, as they unpack this relatable trail phenomenon where faster friends leave you breathless at the peak just as you finally catch up. It is a lighthearted look at pushing your limits, the reality of group dynamics, and why trailing behind might just be the secret to becoming a stronger adventurer.

Then, the energy shifts as special guest and North Queensland adventurer Brock Hawke joins the show to recount an adrenaline-fueled expedition down the Herbert River Gorge. From navigating grade four and five rapids to the intense logistics of carrying 35-kilogram boats through steep terrain, this episode dives deep into the technical skill and pure nerve required for world-class local paddling. Whether you are a hiker or a thrill-seeker, tune in to hear about surviving eight-second swims, avoiding crocodiles, and finding the perfect line in some of North Queensland's most rugged waterways.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/fu-wait-brock-hawke-herbert-river/

Ready to explore the hidden remnants of North Queensland's logging history? Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club legends Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they venture into the heart of Paluma. In this episode, we break down the K-Traverse, a rugged, multi-hour trail steeped in history. Discover the secrets behind this former logging route, including where to spot antique artifacts, beautiful swimming holes like Platypus Lagoon, and the best navigation tips for when the track gets overgrown.

But first, we tackle a truly controversial topic: is Gore-Tex footwear actually a waste of money for North Queensland hikers? Blair, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge hash out the debate between breathability and durability, sharing their go-to shoe recommendations for tackling local creeks and long-distance trails. Whether you are prepping for a big expedition or looking for an accessible walk with the family, this episode is packed with local insights to keep your boots on the ground and your spirits high.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/paluma-k-traverse-and-gore-tex-debate/

Heading out into the North Queensland wilderness is an incredible way to explore, but are you truly prepared for what the trail might throw at you? In this episode of Take a Hike, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to break down the non-negotiables for your hiking backpack. Whether you are a seasoned hiker or planning your first big trek, having the right gear can turn a potential emergency into a manageable situation.

From safety must-haves like Personal Locator Beacons (PLBs) and essential first aid supplies to the nuances of proper hydration and nutrition, we cover it all. Our guests share personal stories and expert advice on handling snake bites, managing leeches, and why you should never rely solely on your mobile phone for navigation in remote bushland. Tune in for practical tips that will ensure you stay safe, comfortable, and ready for your next outdoor escape around Townsville.

In this episode, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to debate the ideal party size for hitting the trails around Townsville. From the serenity of a solo trek to the safety logistics of group hiking and the reality of navigating large school camps, the team breaks down the magic number for a great day out in nature.

We also welcome back local adventurer Brock Hawke to dive deep into the intense world of adventure racing. After being inspired by an international documentary, Brock shares his journey from local Townsville trails to competing in grueling, multi-day endurance events like the Godzone race in New Zealand. Do you love getting outdoors but struggle to find the perfect trail to share with your four-legged best friend? In this episode of Take a Hike, host Blair Woodcock is joined by Townsville Bushwalking Club regulars Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to crack the code on pet-friendly adventuring in North Queensland. We discuss the best spots to explore, the importance of trail etiquette, and how to balance your dog's energy levels with a great day out in the bush.

From the scenic vistas of Mount Stuart and the flat, easy-going tracks around Ross River to hidden gems like the trails near Jensen and coastal walks at Pallarenda, we cover a variety of terrain for every type of hiker. We also dive into essential tips for responsible pet ownership on the trails, including why keeping your dog on a leash and picking up after them is non-negotiable for preserving our natural spaces. Whether you have a high-energy breed or a more relaxed companion, join us as we explore the best ways to enjoy Townsville's beautiful backyard with your furry family members.

Think you know every path on Townsville's iconic Castle Hill? Think again. In this episode of Take a Hike, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to venture beyond the main tourist routes. From the intriguing history of why the area was once known as Castle Town to the lesser-known scrambling routes that offer a different perspective of our city skyline, we reveal why this central landmark remains a treasure trove for local hikers.

Our guests share their personal favorites among the over 20 tracks available, discussing everything from the popular Dianella and Triandra paths to the adrenaline-pumping exposure of the Widowmaker track. We also dive into the importance of track maintenance, the debate over stair installations versus natural terrain, and how these upgrades help preserve the environment for future adventurers. Whether you are a seasoned trail runner or just looking for a new weekend stroll, this conversation is packed with local knowledge to help you explore Townsville's backyard like never before.

Join Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge as they dive into the rugged beauty of North Queensland's backcountry. In this episode, the team from the Townsville Bushwalking Club highlights a unique, off-the-beaten-path adventure to Killymoon Creek. Because this trail is accessed via private property, it offers a rare, once-in-a-lifetime experience for local bushwalkers to explore a stunning, untouched landscape just a short drive from Townsville.

Beyond the swimming holes and creek hopping of Killymoon, the conversation turns to the history hidden atop nearby Saddle Mountain. The team discusses the fascinating rediscovery of a World War II plane crash site nestled near the summit, sharing stories of the engine debris and the memorial left behind. Whether you are looking for an introductory hike or a deep dive into local history, this episode provides all the inspiration you need to plan your next weekend in the bush.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/discover-killymoon-wwii-plane-crash/

Ready to dive into one of the most stunning spots in the Paluma Range National Park? Join hosts Cherry Judge and Luen Warneke from the Townsville Bush Walking Club as they take you on a virtual trek to Crystal Creek Falls. In this episode, we break down everything you need to know about this picturesque, beginner-friendly hike. From the best way to get there and where to park, to secret detours like the prehistoric boulders of Torsten's Rock Garden and Wilfred's Lookout, we have you covered. Whether you are hunting for the perfect swimming hole or just want to escape into nature, this walk is a must-do for any local adventurer.

Beyond the trail guide, we discuss essential safety tips, including why you should pack a snake bandage, how to choose the right hiking footwear, and the importance of carrying a personal locator beacon (PLB) when heading off the grid. We also touch on the Leave No Trace philosophy to help ensure these beautiful trails remain pristine for years to come. Plus, stick around for our advice on local canyoning opportunities and how to join us for an upcoming club hike. Grab your gear and get ready to hit the trail!

Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club legends Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they take a deep dive into one of the world's most spectacular multi-day hiking experiences: Hinchinbrook Island. Known to many as the home of the iconic Thorsborne Trail, this rugged North Queensland gem offers everything from pristine beaches and hidden swimming holes to challenging mountain peaks. Whether you are a seasoned trekker or planning your first multi-day adventure, this episode is packed with the insights you need to make the most of your trip.

In this episode, we break down the logistics of the 32-kilometre trail, share tips on securing those elusive camping permits, and discuss the best approach for both beginners and experienced hikers. We also cover essential topics like safety in the wilderness, the importance of respecting Leave No Trace principles, and the hidden gems you can find if you are willing to explore a little further off the beaten track. From the stunning views at Nina Peak to the refreshing waters of Zoe Bay, discover why Hinchinbrook remains a must-visit destination for any hiking enthusiast.

Take a Hike to Alligator Creek and disco.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Hiking the Reopened Alligator Creek Falls & Secret Gorge
Take a Hike to Alligator Creek and disco.spotify_description:
After being closed for three long years, the highly anticipated Alligator Creek Falls track in Bowling Green National Park, just south of Townsville, is finally open! Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club experts Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they unpack everything you need to know about this spectacular 17km full-day hike. Whether you're tackling it on foot or taking the steep mountain bike route via the old Telstra Tower Track, this adventure is back on the menu.


Expect multiple creek crossings - some potentially chest-deep after heavy rain - before reaching the stunning main falls. The team discusses the best times to tackle the exposed firebreak sections and the shady rainforest trail that follows. For the brave and sure-footed, Luen and Cherry reveal the unofficial path up to the spectacular infinity pools at the top of the falls, offering incredible views across Saddle Mountain.


Not up for a 17km epic? The crew also shares a little-known secret spot called "the Gorge", located just a few hundred meters upstream from the main car park. It's the perfect private waterhole for families or anyone looking to beat the crowds without the long trek. Tune in to hear all the tips and tricks for making the most of your next Townsville bushwalking adventure. You can find more details on our blog: [https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/alligator-creek-falls-reopened/](https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/alligator-creek-falls-reopened/)


Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/alligator-creek-falls-reopened/

Join us on a journey to one of North Queensland's most spectacular hidden gems. In this episode, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Cherry Judge and Luen Warneke from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to pull back the curtain on the iconic Terraces at Mount Elliot in Bowling Green Bay National Park. Known for its mesmerizing rock formations and cascading waterfalls, this destination is a must-see for local adventurers, though it remains a challenging, off-track experience.

We discuss the logistics of exploring this pristine wilderness, including why access through private property makes joining an organized group like the Townsville Bushwalking Club the best way to ensure you can visit. We also touch on the history of the site, safety tips for this grade five hike, and how you can get involved with the club to explore these stunning locations responsibly. Whether you are a seasoned bushwalker or looking to discover new trails near Townsville, this episode is your guide to experiencing the wonders of our backyard.

Join hosts Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge as they tackle one of North Queensland's most iconic and challenging peaks: Mount Halifax. Standing tall at 1063 metres in the Paluma Range National Park, this mountain offers everything from beginner-friendly swims at Rope Falls to a grueling, full-day trek for the seasoned hiker. Whether you are looking for a quick dip in a stunning waterhole or an intense climb featuring scree slopes and rope scrambles, this episode breaks down everything you need to know about the trail.

Beyond the physical challenge, Luen and Cherry share the fascinating history of the trail, originally cut by Vietnam veterans training for the Kokoda Track. The team covers essential safety tips, from packing gloves for the rope sections to understanding the importance of Leave No Trace principles in our delicate wilderness. Grab your boots, plan your day, and get ready to experience the incredible views and lush rainforests that await on this Townsville bucket-list adventure.

Ready to take your hiking to the next level? In this episode, host Blair Woodcock is joined by Townsville Bushwalking Club regulars Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to tackle the ultimate challenge: Mount Bartle Frere. As the highest mountain in Queensland, this peak is a must-do for any serious adventurer looking to test their limits.

We break down everything you need to know before you lace up your boots, including the differences between the challenging Eastern approach via Josephine Falls and the more accessible Western route. From navigating the famous granite boulder fields and dealing with the infamous local leeches to planning for water supplies and choosing the best season to climb, we share our best tips for a successful summit. Whether you are planning a single-day push or an overnight camping adventure, join us as we explore how to prepare for this iconic North Queensland climb.

Take a Hike, but you sometimes unwelcome.spotify_description:
Are you planning a North Queensland adventure but dreading the thought of uninvited guests on your ankles? In this episode of Take a Hike, your host Blair Woodcock sits down with Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge to swap stories from the trail and tackle the slimy reality of hiking during the wet season. From unexpected encounters in Paluma to bizarre tales of leeches in the eye, we share our most memorable experiences from the bush.

Beyond the horror stories, we break down the best, safest, and most effective ways to remove leeches without causing infection or unnecessary irritation. We also discuss practical prevention strategies, including gear tips and the pros and cons of using repellents like DEET. Whether you are a seasoned trekker or new to exploring the beautiful trails around Townsville, this guide will help you manage these tiny hikers so you can focus on the stunning waterfalls and scenery instead.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/dealing-with-leeches-hiking-tips/

Take a hike and discover some secret sea.spotify_title: Chasing Waterfalls: Finding Townsville's Hidden Seasonal Gems
Take a hike and discover some secret sea.spotify_description:
Rainy weather in North Queensland brings more than just mud-it brings spectacular, fleeting waterfalls to life! Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club experts Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they discuss how to make the most of the wet season. From the popular and accessible Endeavour Falls on Magnetic Island to the mysterious 'Dog Shed' waterhole near West Point, the team shares where to find the best local cascades while emphasizing the importance of safety when exploring these natural swimming spots.

Beyond the island, the group explores mainland favorites like the stunning Bridal Falls on Hervey's Range and highlights upcoming adventures with the Townsville Bushwalking Club, including an off-track expedition near Wallaman Falls and a mystery hike at Bluegum Creek. Whether you are a seasoned hiker or looking for a scenic drive, this episode is packed with tips and inspiration to get you out on the trails. For more information on upcoming club activities and maps, head to townsvillebushwalkingclub.com or check out wanderstories.space.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/discover-secret-seasonal-waterfalls-around-townsville/

Take a hike and explore Gold Creek in th.spotify_title: Explore the Wilds of Gold Creek: Paluma National Park Hiking Adventures
Take a hike and explore Gold Creek in th.spotify_description:
Ready to head back up the range? Join Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge as they dive into the lush, off-track beauty of Gold Creek in Paluma National Park. In this episode, the team discusses the unique water divergence where Gold Creek meets Cairn Creek, the mossy landscapes created by recent rain, and the best ways to explore this remote terrain safely.

Whether you are a seasoned trekker or looking to build your bushwalking skills, the crew shares essential tips on navigation, the importance of going with experienced companions, and how to safely transition from on-track hiking to off-track exploration. Tune in for expert advice on utilizing the Townsville Bushwalking Club, using GPS tools, and planning your next big adventure in North Queensland's breathtaking national parks.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/exploring-gold-creek-paluma/

Take a hike and explore The Bambaroo Tra.spotify_title: Explore the Bambaroo Track: Giant Fig Trees and Hidden Waterfalls
Take a hike and explore The Bambaroo Tra.spotify_description:
Looking for your next North Queensland adventure? Join Blair Woodcock, Cherry Judge, and Luen Warneke as they take a deep dive into the hidden gem that is the Bambaroo Track. Located just an hour north of Townsville, this trail is a spectacular mix of diverse terrain, from stunning rainforest vegetation and massive, ancient fig trees to historical remnants of old mining sites. Whether you are a casual hiker or a seasoned explorer, this trail offers something truly special right in our own backyard.

In this episode, we break down everything you need to know about navigating the Bambaroo Track. Discover the secrets behind finding Fig Tree Falls and the rugged, beautiful gorge at Insulator Creek, and learn why this track is the perfect training ground for those looking to level up their fitness for overnight hikes. Tune in for expert tips on safety, navigation, and why a stop at Frosty Mangoes is the ultimate post-hike reward.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/take-a-hike-bambaroo-track/

LISTNR - TAKE A HIKE EP 1.mp3.spotify_title: Discover Crystal Falls: Townsville Bushwalking & Paluma Hiking Adventures
LISTNR - TAKE A HIKE EP 1.mp3.spotify_description:
Welcome to Take A Hike, your guide to the best bushwalking and hiking adventures in and around Townsville, Australia! This week, Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge from the Townsville Bushwalking Club share the rich history of their organization, which has been pioneering local trails for over 60 years. Discover how their strong relationships with local landowners open up a world of spectacular views and hidden emerald swimming holes that you won't find on standard tourist maps.


In this episode, the team takes you on a journey to the stunning Crystal Falls in Paluma. Perfect for beginners and families, this half-day return hike starts at Paluma Dam and features breathtaking stops like Wilfred's Lookout. Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge share essential tips for navigating the track, including the importance of downloading offline maps from Paluma.org, as mobile signal is non-existent. You'll also hear why the crystal-clear waters of this year-round swimming hole are totally worth the trek.


For those looking for a bigger challenge, learn how to extend your hike along the historic Bullocky Toms track. Rich in 1870s tin mining history, this full-day adventure offers medium effort for incredibly high rewards, including dual waterfalls and panoramic lookouts. Remember to always hike safe, carry a PLB and first aid kit, and leave no trace. Check out the episode notes for a link to our complete Crystal Falls hiking guide!


Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/crystal-falls-paluma-hiking-guide/

Take a Hike - Snake 101 & The Dalrymple.spotify_title: Townsville Bushwalking: Snake 101 and Hiking Dalrymple Gap
Take a Hike - Snake 101 & The Dalrymple.youtube_description:
Ready to hit the trails around North Queensland? In this podcast, Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge talk about the unexpected dangers and stunning beauty of bushwalking near Townsville. The crew kicks things off with a wild story about a python attack in Behana Gorge, sharing essential tips on snake etiquette, heavy footfalls, and why you might want to invest in a good pair of gaiters.

Next, the team dives into the Dalrymple Gap track, a beautiful 10km historical hike located just south of Cardwell. Discover why starting from the Cardwell side is the best route, learn about the historic stone bridge built with bricks shipped all the way from Scotland, and find out what to watch out for on the trail (including the notorious wait-a-while and stinging trees).

Whether you are a beginner looking for a scenic day trip or training for a massive adventure, this beautiful rainforest track has something for everyone. Lace up your boots, grab your walking sticks, and join the Townsville Bushwalking Club crew for another great guide to the outdoors.

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/

#TakeAHike #Townsville #Bushwalking #Hiking #NorthQueensland #DalrympleGap #SnakeSafety #Cardwell #QueenslandHiking #TownsvilleBushwalkingClub #OutdoorAdventure #TrailWalking
Take a Hike - Snake 101 & The Dalrymple.spotify_description:
Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge are back to discuss the wild side of North Queensland hiking! We kick things off with a crazy story about a recent python encounter in Behana Gorge. Are non-venomous snakes something to worry about? The team shares essential snake etiquette for the trails, from heavy footfalls and thick gaiters to using walking sticks to stay safe in the Australian bush.

Next, we head north of Townsville for a deep dive into the Dalrymple Gap walking track. This 10km historical bushwalking route just south of Cardwell offers beautiful rainforest views, stunning creek crossings, and an incredible bridge built with bricks shipped all the way from Scotland. The crew debates the best way to tackle the route - starting from the Cardwell side to get the steep hill out of the way, or braving the Broadwater side for the beautiful creeks.

Plus, we give you a heads-up on native trail hazards like the annoying wait-a-while vine and the dreaded gimpy-gimpy stinging tree, and chat about upcoming weekend adventures to the Russell River area and Diorite Falls. Grab your gaiters, lace up your boots, and get ready for another fantastic bushwalking episode! Read more about this route on our blog at [https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/](https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/)

Read the full blog post: https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/
